Alliaria turczaninowii, also known as Turczaninow's garlic mustard, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of various diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders. The bioactive constituents of Alliaria turczaninowii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
